{{Quote|It has been far too long since the Sith have had an academy on Korriban.|Qordis after the Brotherhood of Darkness took Korriban|<ref name="PoD" />}}
'''Qordis''' (or '''Lord Eddels''' to the banks) was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] who was one of the highest-ranking members of the [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]] and a supporter of [[Skere Kaan]]. A tall, gaunt Human male with a cruel demeanor and a taste for luxury, Qordis was founder and headmaster of the [[Sith Academy (Korriban)|Sith Academy]] on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] during the final years of the [[New Sith Wars]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|Honor is for the living. Dead is dead.|Darth Bane, as he killed Qordis|<ref name="PoD" />}}
Qordis was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] who was one of the strongest [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]], along with [[Kopecz]] and [[Kas'im]], who pledged themselves to [[Skere Kaan|Kaan's]] [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]]. Qordis was involved in [[Sith training|training]] a Human man named [[Darth Bane/Legends|Bane]] at the [[Sith Academy (Korriban)|Sith Academy]] on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]. Qordis's rivalry with Kopecz, who had brought Bane to the Academy, distanced him from the young apprentice, and possibly brought him to dislike him.<ref name="PoD" />

After Bane entered the Sith Academy, it was rumored among the students that Qordis had been training the powerful [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Sirak]] for as long as twenty [[Standard year/Legends|years]].
When Bane defeated Sirak in a [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|lightsaber duel]], Qordis forbade Bane from his private studies with [[Githany]] and the archives. Bane, however, angrily confronted him, and stormed off to the [[Valley of the Dark Lords/Legends|Valley of the Dark Lords]], where he believed he would be able to learn of the ancient Sith's power. Qordis initially expected him to [[Death/Legends|die]], but [[Kas'im]] convinced him that Bane would return. Upon his return, Qordis presented Bane with a synthetic crystal which he claimed would be more powerful than the one powering the lightsaber presented to Bane by Kas'im and also that of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. Qordis had also promoted Bane to the rank of [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] as part of Kaan's orders and in an attempt to bring Bane back to the Brotherhood. Bane, however, openly defied Qordis; he declared that the Brotherhood was weak and Kaan was leading the Sith down the path of ruin, and he claimed the title of [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] for himself. He took Qordis's ship, the ''[[Valcyn/Legends|Valcyn]]'', and traveled to [[Lehon/Legends|Lehon]].<ref name="PoD" />

Days later, after killing Kas'im and surviving being poisoned by Githany, Bane headed to [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]]. There, Qordis saw the power his student was able to unleash and approached him clandestinely, petitioning him to overthrow Kaan and take control of the Brotherhood. Qordis claimed that since many of the former students from the Korriban Academy still looked up to him, he could be of assistance by convincing them to pledge their loyalty to Bane as the new leader of the Brotherhood. Bane, who planned to destroy the Brotherhood, was disgusted at the very idea of controlling it. He used his powers to lift Qordis in the air and slowly clenched his fist, crushing the Sith's body and denying him an "honorable" death in battle, as Qordis had requested.<ref name="PoD" />

===Legacy===
Bane would later learn that Qordis had embezzled funds from the Brotherhood to help pay for his avarice, and that he used the [[InterGalactic Banking Clan/Legends|InterGalactic Banking Clan]] to store the funds under the name [[Lord Eddels]], which Bane would later tap into. Sometime during Qordis's life, he acquired the [[Scroll of Freedon Nadd]] and made notes within containing the location of the [[Tomb of Freedon Nadd]], which, much like his embezzled funds, came into the hands of Bane after being found by his apprentice, [[Darth Zannah/Legends|Darth Zannah]].<ref name="RoT">''[[Darth Bane: Rule of Two]]''</ref>

Qordis would later appear to Bane as part of his imagination caused by damage done to his brain by the [[thought bomb]]. On [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon's]] moon, [[Dxun/Legends|Dxun]], this would cause Bane to lash out with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] and critically damage the ''Valcyn'', resulting in Bane's near death.<ref name="RoT" />

As Bane emerged from Nadd's tomb, Qordis no longer bothered Bane as Bane had symbolically vanquished both Qordis's and Kaan's spirits from his healed mind.<ref name="RoT" />

==Personality and traits==
Qordis was a cunning and manipulative Sith Lord, but was also known for his pettiness. He was willing to let Bane's talent go to waste and destroy himself merely because it was Kopecz who brought Bane to the Academy. Even after death, he was willing to kill Bane, the last member of his order, out of spite.<ref name="PoD" />

Qordis surrounded himself with wealth, with the intention of making others envious. He also had no qualms about stealing from the Brotherhood that he had sworn loyalty to, merely to line his own coffers.<ref name="PoD" />

At the Academy he was known for his favoritism, holding Sirak in high regard. He dismissed the past and viewed himself and the Brotherhood as the only way of achieving the full power of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]].<ref name="PoD" />

Qordis was even taller than Bane, with talon-like nails and sharp teeth. He had an unnatural chalky complexion. He was usually clad in a dark robe that hung off him.<ref name="PoD" />
